items.back: 1. MEARTS-based radar surveillance. 2. Conflict Prediction and Reporting. 3. ADS-B. 4. ADS-C. 5. CPDLC. 6. AIDC. 7. Weather Deviation Tool. 8. Additional Decision Support Tools (situational awareness). 9. Electronic Flight Data including Electronic Flight Strips. Per 13-2-1(b).

items.front: ATOP: what four situations allow overriding conflict probe?
items.back: 1. Separation standard not recognized by probe. 2. Action taken to resolve and separation ensured. 3. Control delegated to another sector/facility. 4. Other situations specified in facility directives. Controllers must continue to ensure separation until overridden conflict is resolved. Per 13-2-2(a)(3).

Once initiated, trial probe does NOT account for any subsequent changes. It is a snapshot at the time of entry. Does NOT relieve controller from performing conflict probe when issuing clearance or accepting transfer. Per 13-2-2(a)(6) NOTE.

items.front: ATOP additional decision support tools: when can they set separation?
items.back: Only when the separation standard is NOT supported by probe or when probe is UNAVAILABLE. NEVER use them to override supported probe results unless an authorized override condition applies. Per 13-2-2(b)(1-2).

items.front: ATOP: four reduced separation flags on electronic flight strips
items.back: M = Mach Number Technique (MNT). R = Reduced MNT. D = Distance-based longitudinal. W = Reduced Vertical Separation Minimum (RVSM). Per 13-2-3(c)(1)(b).

items.front: ATOP: when to select Degraded RNP on the electronic flight strip
items.back: When the aircraft notifies ATC of a reduction in navigation capability that affects the applicable separation minima. Per 13-2-3(c)(1)(c).

items.front: CPDLC: when it is primary means of communication
items.back: When CPDLC is AVAILABLE and the CPDLC-connected aircraft is operating OUTSIDE VHF COVERAGE. Must is used — it is mandatory. Per 13-2-4(a)(1).

items.front: CPDLC: required voice backup and abnormal conditions
items.back: Voice backup (HF, SATCOM, Third party) MUST exist. If CPDLC fails, use voice. If service cancelled, advise pilot ASAP. Lost connection: send CR1 or advise log-on. Uncertain clearance delivery: continue protecting airspace. Per 13-2-4(a)(3), (c).

items.front: CPDLC failure: initial voice contact phraseology
